entries.title: 2 Chronicles 2:4
entries.author: John Wesley (1703–1791)
entries.content: Erred - If I have sinned, I myself suffer for my sins, and therefore deserve your pity rather than reproaches.

entries.title: 2 Chronicles 2:9
entries.author: John Wesley (1703–1791)
entries.content: Every side - In all respects, my person, and family, and estate. Gone - I am a lost and dead man. Hope - All my hopes of the present life, but not of the life to come. Tree - Which being once plucked up by the roots, never grows again. Hope in this life is a perishing thing. But the hope of good men, when it is cut off from this world, is but removed like a tree, transplanted from this nursery to the garden of God.
